After [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=}} * [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] * [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] * [[:en:Nishio Tadanao|Nishio Tadanao]] == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanda_Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] * {{ill|Sansha Festival Music|ja|三社祭礼囃子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansha Festival Music|qq=}} * [[:en:Sannō Matsuri|Sannō Festival (Tokyo)]] * {{ill|Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|水神宮 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|ja|江岳寺 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/ Official Website of Mikumano Shrine] ''' {{Instagram|mikumanojinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|三熊野神社 (掛川市)|version=104217640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Mikumano Shrine (Kakegawa)]] [[sn:E4%B8%89%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%8E%9B%E5%B7%9D%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:三熊野神社]] [[Category:701 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Emperor Monmu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11356650]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | name = Mikumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:三熊野神社（鳥居・拝殿）.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]] Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near [[:en:Yokosuka Castle|Yokosuka Castle]]. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|qq=}} in [[:en:Omaezaki|Omaezaki]] and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. After [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=}} * [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] * [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] * [[:en:Nishio Tadanao|Nishio Tadanao]] == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanda_Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] * {{ill|Sansha Festival Music|ja|三社祭礼囃子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansha Festival Music|qq=}} * [[:en:Sannō Matsuri|Sannō Festival (Tokyo)]] * {{ill|Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|水神宮 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|ja|江岳寺 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/ Official Website of Mikumano Shrine] ''' {{Instagram|mikumanojinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|三熊野神社 (掛川市)|version=104217640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Mikumano Shrine (Kakegawa)]] [[sn:E4%B8%89%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%8E%9B%E5%B7%9D%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:三熊野神社]] [[Category:701 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Emperor Monmu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=}} * [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] * [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] * [[:en:Nishio Tadanao|Nishio Tadanao]] == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanda_Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] * {{ill|Sansha Festival Music|ja|三社祭礼囃子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansha Festival Music|qq=}} * [[:en:Sannō Matsuri|Sannō Festival (Tokyo)]] * {{ill|Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|水神宮 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|ja|江岳寺 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/ Official Website of Mikumano Shrine] ''' {{Instagram|mikumanojinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|三熊野神社 (掛川市)|version=104217640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Mikumano Shrine (Kakegawa)]] [[sn:E4%B8%89%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%8E%9B%E5%B7%9D%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:三熊野神社]] [[Category:701 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Emperor Monmu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11356650]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Mikumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:三熊野神社（鳥居・拝殿）.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]] Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near [[:en:Yokosuka Castle|Yokosuka Castle]]. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|qq=}} in [[:en:Omaezaki|Omaezaki]] and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. After [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=}} * [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] * [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] * [[:en:Nishio Tadanao|Nishio Tadanao]] == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanda_Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] * {{ill|Sansha Festival Music|ja|三社祭礼囃子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansha Festival Music|qq=}} * [[:en:Sannō Matsuri|Sannō Festival (Tokyo)]] * {{ill|Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|水神宮 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|ja|江岳寺 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/ Official Website of Mikumano Shrine] ''' {{Instagram|mikumanojinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|三熊野神社 (掛川市)|version=104217640|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kumano shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Mikumano Shrine (Kakegawa)]] [[sn:E4%B8%89%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%8E%9B%E5%B7%9D%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:三熊野神社]] [[Category:701 establishments]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Emperor Monmu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near [[:en:Yokosuka Castle|Yokosuka Castle]]. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|qq=|1=Takamatsu Jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in [[:en:Omaezaki|Omaezaki]] and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. After [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin|comment=changed title based on {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11356650]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Mikumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:三熊野神社（鳥居・拝殿）.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]] Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near [[:en:Yokosuka Castle|Yokosuka Castle]]. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|qq=|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in [[:en:Omaezaki|Omaezaki]] and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. After [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1753428}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} * [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] * [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] * [[:en:Nishio Tadanao|Nishio Tadanao]] == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanda_Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] * {{ill|Sansha Festival Music|ja|三社祭礼囃子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansha Festival Music|qq=|1=Sanja Sairei Bayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356852}} * [[:en:Sannō Matsuri|Sannō Festival (Tokyo)]] * {{ill|Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|水神宮 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|qq=|1=Suijingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549048}} * {{ill|Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|ja|江岳寺 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/ Official Website of Mikumano Shrine] ''' {{Instagram|mikumanojinja}} Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near [[:en:Yokosuka Castle|Yokosuka Castle]]. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|qq=|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in [[:en:Omaezaki|Omaezaki]] and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. After [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin|comment=changed title based on {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11356650]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Mikumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:三熊野神社（鳥居・拝殿）.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]] Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near [[:en:Yokosuka Castle|Yokosuka Castle]]. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|qq=|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in [[:en:Omaezaki|Omaezaki]] and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. After [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1753428}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} * [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] * [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] * [[:en:Nishio Tadanao|Nishio Tadanao]] == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanda_Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] * {{ill|Sansha Festival Music|ja|三社祭礼囃子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansha Festival Music|qq=|1=Sanja Sairei Bayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356852}} * [[:en:Sannō Matsuri|Sannō Festival (Tokyo)]] * {{ill|Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|水神宮 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|qq=|1=Suijingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549048}} * {{ill|Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|ja|江岳寺 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/ Official Website of Mikumano Shrine] ''' {{Instagram|mikumanojinja}} Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near [[:en:Yokosuka Castle|Yokosuka Castle]]. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|qq=|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in [[:en:Omaezaki|Omaezaki]] and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. After [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11356650]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Mikumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:三熊野神社（鳥居・拝殿）.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]] Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near [[:en:Yokosuka Castle|Yokosuka Castle]]. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|qq=|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in [[:en:Omaezaki|Omaezaki]] and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. After [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1753428}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11356650]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Mikumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:三熊野神社（鳥居・拝殿）.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]] Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near [[:en:Yokosuka Castle|Yokosuka Castle]]. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|qq=|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in [[:en:Omaezaki|Omaezaki]] and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. After [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1753428}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]] * {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} * [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]] * [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] * [[:en:Nishio Tadanao|Nishio Tadanao]] == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Kanda_Matsuri|Kanda Matsuri]] * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] * {{ill|Sansha Festival Music|ja|三社祭礼囃子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansha Festival Music|qq=|1=Sanja Sairei Bayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356852}} * [[:en:Sannō Matsuri|Sannō Festival (Tokyo)]] * {{ill|Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|水神宮 (掛川市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|qq=|1=Suijingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549048}} * {{ill|Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|ja|江岳寺 "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin|comment=changed title based on
