[[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11356650]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Mikumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:三熊野神社（鳥居・拝殿）.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}} Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near {{ill|Yokosuka Castle|en|Yokosuka Castle|WD=Q2971080|lt=Yokosuka Castle}}. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}}. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in {{ill|Omaezaki|en|Omaezaki|WD=Q850380|lt=Omaezaki}} and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi}} and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}}. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}, prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. After {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1753428}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} * {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} * {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} * {{ill|Nishio Tadanao|en|Nishio Tadanao|WD=Q11627751|lt=Nishio Tadanao}} == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanda_Matsuri|en|Kanda_Matsuri|WD=Q218646|lt=Kanda Matsuri}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha}} * {{ill|Sansha Festival Music|ja|三社祭礼囃子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansha Festival Music|1=Sanja Sairei Bayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356852}} * {{ill|Sannō Matsuri|en|Sannō Matsuri|WD=Q218663|lt=Sannō Festival (Tokyo)}} * {{ill|Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|水神宮 (掛川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|1=Suijingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549048}} * {{ill|Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|ja|江岳寺 (掛川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/ Official Website of Mikumano Shrine] ''' {{Instagram|mikumanojinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|三熊野神社 (掛川市)|version=104217640|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11356651}} [[Category:701 establishments]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Monmu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kumano shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

"[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. (掛川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/ Official Website of Mikumano Shrine] ''' {{Instagram|mikumanojinja}} {{Shinto revision.text:
[[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Mikumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:三熊野神社（鳥居・拝殿）.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}} Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near {{ill|Yokosuka Castle|en|Yokosuka Castle|WD=Q2971080|lt=Yokosuka Castle}}. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}}. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in {{ill|Omaezaki|en|Omaezaki|WD=Q850380|lt=Omaezaki}} and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi}} and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}}. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}, prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. After {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1753428}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} * {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} * {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} * {{ill|Nishio Tadanao|en|Nishio Tadanao|WD=Q11627751|lt=Nishio Tadanao}} == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanda_Matsuri|en|Kanda_Matsuri|WD=Q218646|lt=Kanda Matsuri}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha}} * {{ill|Sansha Festival Music|ja|三社祭礼囃子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansha Festival Music|1=Sanja Sairei Bayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356852}} * {{ill|Sannō Matsuri|en|Sannō Matsuri|WD=Q218663|lt=Sannō Festival (Tokyo)}} * {{ill|Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|水神宮 (掛川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|1=Suijingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549048}} * {{ill|Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|ja|江岳寺 (掛川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/ Official Website of Mikumano Shrine] ''' {{Instagram|mikumanojinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|三熊野神社 (掛川市)|version=104217640|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11356651}} [[Category:701 establishments]] [[Category:Completion revision.text:
[[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Mikumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:三熊野神社（鳥居・拝殿）.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}} Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near {{ill|Yokosuka Castle|en|Yokosuka Castle|WD=Q2971080|lt=Yokosuka Castle}}. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}}. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in {{ill|Omaezaki|en|Omaezaki|WD=Q850380|lt=Omaezaki}} and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi}} and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}}. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}, prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. After {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1753428}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} * {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} * {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} * {{ill|Nishio Tadanao|en|Nishio Tadanao|WD=Q11627751|lt=Nishio Tadanao}} == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanda_Matsuri|en|Kanda_Matsuri|WD=Q218646|lt=Kanda Matsuri}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha}} * {{ill|Sansha Festival Music|ja|三社祭礼囃子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansha Festival Music|1=Sanja Sairei Bayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356852}} * {{ill|Sannō Matsuri|en|Sannō Matsuri|WD=Q218663|lt=Sannō Festival (Tokyo)}} * {{ill|Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|水神宮 (掛川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|1=Suijingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549048}} * {{ill|Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|ja|江岳寺 (掛川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/ Official Website of Mikumano Shrine] ''' {{Instagram|mikumanojinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|三熊野神社 (掛川市)|version=104217640|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11356651}} [[Category:701 establishments]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Monmu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kumano shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Her prayer was answered with the birth of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}, prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Torii and Haiden | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}} Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near {{ill|Yokosuka Castle|en|Yokosuka Castle|WD=Q2971080|lt=Yokosuka Castle}}. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}}. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in {{ill|Omaezaki|en|Omaezaki|WD=Q850380|lt=Omaezaki}} and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi}} and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}}. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}, prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. After {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1753428}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} * {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}} * {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} * {{ill|Nishio Tadanao|en|Nishio Tadanao|WD=Q11627751|lt=Nishio Tadanao}} == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Mikumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:三熊野神社（鳥居・拝殿）.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near {{ill|Yokosuka Castle|en|Yokosuka Castle|WD=Q2971080|lt=Yokosuka Castle|1=Yokosuka Castle}}. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in {{ill|Omaezaki|en|Omaezaki|WD=Q850380|lt=Omaezaki|1=Omaezaki}} and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. After {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin (kami)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1753428}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References == {{reflist}} == Related Figures == * {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}} * {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} * {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} * {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} * {{ill|Nishio Tadanao|en|Nishio Tadanao|WD=Q11627751|lt=Nishio Tadanao|1=Nishio Tadanao}} == See Also == * {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanda_Matsuri|en|Kanda_Matsuri|WD=Q218646|lt=Kanda Matsuri|1=Kanda Matsuri}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha|1=Kumano Hongū Taisha}} * {{ill|Sansha Festival Music|ja|三社祭礼囃子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sansha Festival Music|1=Sanja Sairei Bayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11356852}} * {{ill|Sannō Matsuri|en|Sannō Matsuri|WD=Q218663|lt=Sannō Festival (Tokyo)|1=Sannō Matsuri}} * {{ill|Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|ja|水神宮 (掛川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Shrine (Kakegawa)|1=Suijingū Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549048}} * {{ill|Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|ja|江岳寺 (掛川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgaku-ji (Kakegawa)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/ Official Website of Mikumano Shrine] ''' {{Instagram|mikumanojinja}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|三熊野神社 (掛川市)|version=104217640|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三熊野神社 (掛川市)]] "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. After {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. Torii and Haiden | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} Nishioobuchi 5631-1 | coordinates = {{coord2|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Traditional: Early 8th century (Taibō era) | Main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alternate name = | Annual festival = First Friday of April and the following two days
({{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}) | Rituals = Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Mikumano Shrine|coordinates={{coord|34|40|56.9|N|137|59|0.5|E}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15}} }} '''Mikumano Shrine''' (三熊野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. Known for its crest, the "Right Three Chōji Tomoe," it is associated with the {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} shrines. == Overview == [[File:2rin-yatai-yokosuka.jpg|thumb|200px|Two-wheeled floats at the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}]] The shrine is located near {{ill|Yokosuka Castle|en|Yokosuka Castle|WD=Q2971080|lt=Yokosuka Castle|1=Yokosuka Castle}}. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670486}} in {{ill|Omaezaki|en|Omaezaki|WD=Q850380|lt=Omaezaki|1=Omaezaki}} and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. After {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. According to tradition, it was established during the early 8th century (Taibō era) under the orders of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|qid=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. The deities enshrined are {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, who are believed to have been transferred from the Kumano Sanzan shrines. Together with {{ill|Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|ja|高松神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Shrine (Omaezaki)|1=Takamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11670486}} in {{ill|Omaezaki|en|Omaezaki|qid=Q850380|lt=Omaezaki|1=Omaezaki}} and {{ill|Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasa Shrine|lt=Ogasajinja Shrine|ja|小笠神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasajinja Shrine|qq=}} in Kakegawa, Mikumano Shrine is referred to as one of the "Kumano Sanzan of Enshū." == Festivals == === Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival === The annual festival, held over three days starting on the first Friday of April, is a major event. Known as the {{ill|Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|ja|遠州横須賀三熊野神社大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshū Yokosuka Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival|qq=}}, it features processions with {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|qid=Q979940|lt=mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} and the pulling of two-wheeled floats called "Neri" (祢里), drawing large crowds of visitors.Kakegawa City Hall, Commerce, Labor and Tourism Division. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma.jsp Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Retrieved June 9, 2008. === Mikodaki Ceremony (Child-Blessing Ritual) === On the second day of the festival, the Mikodaki Ceremony, or child-blessing ritual, takes place. Participants carry a doll called "Onenneko-sama" as part of the procession. This ritual attracts many visitors seeking blessings for children.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/mikodaki.html | title = Mikodaki Ceremony | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == History == Mikumano Shrine was established during the early 8th century by {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|qid=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. According to tradition, the shrine was built after Empress {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623185}} prayed to the Kumano Sanzan shrines for a safe childbirth. Her prayer was answered with the birth of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, prompting the establishment of three shrines in gratitude.Kakegawa City Hall. "[http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/k_jyoho/mikumanojinja_taisai/mikuma_2.jsp Introduction to the Mikumano Shrine Grand Festival]." Over time, the shrine became a center for {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. In 1335, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|qid=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} prayed for victory and reportedly dedicated a bell gifted by {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|qid=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. After {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} built Yokosuka Castle in 1578, the shrine gained further prominence among the local rulers and residents.{{Cite web | author = Sansha Gongen Mikumano Shrine | url = http://www4.tokai.or.jp/mikuma/about.html | title = About Mikumano Shrine | accessdate = July 31, 2016 }} == Cultural Properties == The following are designated cultural properties of Shizuoka Prefecture and Kakegawa City:Kakegawa City Education Board, Cultural Property Division. "[http://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/siteiitirannhyio.html List of Designated Cultural Properties]." Retrieved September 23, 2011. === Shizuoka Prefecture Designations === * '''Main Hall''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Architecture) * '''44 Ema Paintings''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Paintings) === Kakegawa City Designations === * '''Tengu Mask''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) * '''Komainu''' (Tangible Cultural Property, Sculpture) == Subsidiary Shrines == The shrine grounds also host three subsidiary shrines: * '''Suwa Shrine''' - Deities: {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * '''Tenjin Shrine''' - deity: {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|ja|天満天神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin (kami)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天満大自在天神|1=Tenjin (kami)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1753428}} * '''Okuno Shrine''' - deity: Okuno Saemon Koreyoshi Kyo == References ==
